description: Sentiment vocabulary and phrase patterns drawn from sim racing broadcast commentary, team radio transcripts, coaching channels, and Crew Chief-style spotter databases. Colors avoid flag colors (red, yellow, blue, orange, black) to prevent collisions with race flag indicators.

sentiments.label: Excitement / Positive
sentiments.description: Expressions of genuine excitement, satisfaction, or joy in a moment.
phrases: Oh that's lovely
That is absolutely beautiful
Yes! Get in there!
That felt so good
I love this car
Look at that, perfect
That's the lap right there
Clean as a whistle
I am absolutely flying
This is why I do this
What a feeling, honestly
That exit was chef's kiss
Getting there, getting there
The car is talking to me now
That's the one — that's the move
Absolutely nailed it
That's a statement lap right there
The car came alive in that section
Everything clicked on that one

sentiments.label: Frustration / Negative
sentiments.description: Expressions of frustration, disappointment, or mild anger at mistakes or incidents.
phrases: Oh come on!
That's just embarrassing
What am I doing
No no no no no
That should not have happened
I lost so much time there
That's a completely amateur mistake
I've given that one away
That's my fault, entirely my fault
Absolutely baffling decision
Why did I brake so early
That's just not good enough
Threw it away, completely threw it away
That's the race right there — gone
Gave them the position on a plate
Self-inflicted and I knew it was coming
That corner has taken more from me than I can afford

sentiments.label: Technical / Analytical
sentiments.description: Calm, thoughtful analysis of car behavior, setup, or driving technique. Draws from coaching instructional style and telemetry analysis language.
phrases: So what's happening here is...
The issue is the rear is stepping out under power
You can feel the front pushing in the slow stuff
The key to this corner is committing early
I need to soften the rear anti-roll bar for this
Trail braking is absolutely critical here
The tire's over the peak temperature now
I'm carrying too much speed into the apex
The understeer is killing my exit speed
You want to rotate the car before the apex
It's all about getting the weight transfer right
I'm losing two tenths in sector two every lap
The setup change made it more neutral
Brake bias is too far forward for this corner
The telemetry shows exactly where I'm losing time
The minimum speed through that corner tells the whole story
Weight transfer from braking is doing all the rotation work
The data doesn't lie — the inputs need to be smoother
Compare the throttle traces and you see the difference immediately

sentiments.label: Self-Deprecating Humor
sentiments.description: Light, relatable humor about one's own mistakes or limitations.
phrases: Absolutely sensational from the Pride of wherever
That is world-class incompetence right there
Please ignore what just happened
I have no idea what I'm doing, and I've accepted that
My driving coach would have a heart attack watching this
That move was absolute madness and I regret it
I am the worst driver in any lobby I enter
If anyone from a real team is watching, I apologize
That looked intentional and it absolutely was not
My brain said brake later and my body said no
I want to clarify that I practice before recording
That corner has humiliated me three sessions running
I should not be allowed near this car unsupervised
This is exactly the kind of content nobody should learn from

sentiments.label: Car Praise
sentiments.description: Expressions of appreciation for a specific car's character or behavior.
phrases: This car is an absolute weapon
The feedback from this thing is incredible
I don't know who built this car but I love them
It sounds absolutely epic
The way it rotates is just gorgeous
You can feel every bit of grip through the wheel
The braking in this thing is just insane
It rewards commitment so well
The engineers absolutely nailed the aerodynamics on this
The balance is just right — neutral, responsive, alive
This thing talks to you through the wheel and rewards precision
You can tell the physics model captured this car properly

sentiments.label: Sim Comparison
sentiments.description: Comparisons between different simulators, often in terms of realism or feel.
phrases: iRacing does this better than any other sim
Assetto Corsa Competizione nails this feeling
This is where this sim falls short for me
Compared to real life, this sim gets it pretty much right
The tire model here is streets ahead of the competition
This is what I mean when I say this sim is the benchmark
I've driven the real car and this is frighteningly accurate
The physics are brilliant but the sound needs work
Every sim has compromises and this one is pretty minor

sentiments.label: Neutral Narrative
sentiments.description: Calm play-by-play commentary and situational narration. Incorporates broadcast commentary structure and race engineer communication patterns.
phrases: So we're coming up to the back straight now
I'm going to be very careful here in traffic
Pit window should be opening in the next couple of laps
The gap ahead is about a second and a half
I'm on the medium compound, should have enough to get to the end
We're about halfway through the stint now
I need to manage the gap to the car behind
Track conditions are improving lap by lap
The safety car is deployed, everyone bunches up
I'm going to try the late apex approach here
The field is strung out and the gaps are stable
Running in clean air now, this is where we build the pace
Car ahead is closing, setting up for the battle
The undercut window is open — the call has to come soon
Fuel numbers are good, we're on plan

sentiments.label: Driving Advice
sentiments.description: Tips and technique advice. Draws from coaching channels (Driver61, Suellio Almeida) and structured driving instruction patterns.
phrases: If you're learning this corner, the key is patience on the entry
Trail braking is what separates fast drivers from very fast drivers
Don't be afraid to use the full width of the track
You want to be looking at the exit before you hit the apex
The biggest gain for most people is braking technique
Smooth inputs are faster than aggressive inputs, always
I'd recommend dialing in your FOV before anything else
Setup changes are meaningless if your driving isn't consistent
The throttle on corner exit is where time is really made
Brake in a straight line first, then trail into the corner as you turn
Focus on one corner per session until it becomes automatic
The reference points don't change — commit to them every lap
You're overdriving it — back it up two percent and the lap time comes

sentiments.label: Spotter / Proximity
sentiments.description: Short, urgent proximity and safety calls inspired by Crew Chief spotter databases and race engineer communication. Used for close racing, flag changes, and immediate hazard warnings.
phrases: Car left, hold your line
Car right, stay tight
Clear all around
Three wide into the corner
They're closing fast from behind
You've got the inside, commit to it
Gap is under a second — they're pushing
Debris ahead, stay left
Incident ahead, back it up
Clear to rejoin, watch traffic

sentiments.label: Race Engineer / Strategy
sentiments.description: Strategy-focused communication in the style of F1 team radio and race engineer calls. Covers fuel management, tyre strategy, gap reports, and session management.
phrases: Box this lap, box this lap
Fuel is marginal — lift and coast into the slow corners
Gap to car ahead is stable at one point two
You're faster in sector two, that's where the move is
Tyres are in the window, push now while you have the grip
The pace is good, keep this rhythm going
We're on the alternate strategy, trust the numbers
The undercut is working — three tenths quicker out of the stop
Conditions changing — rain on the radar, stay on plan for now
Pace is dropping, consider your options for the next few laps
